---
title: "Standardize Your Data"
topics:
  - Workflows
  - Standards & Vocabulary
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
---

```{=html}
<div class="status-badge-row">
  <span class="status-badge status-active">Active Workflow</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-canonical">Canonical Terms Required</span>
  <span class="status-badge status-guidance">Package-first</span>
</div>
```

Use this page when you need the **shortest correct path** from a messy source dataset to a standards-aligned salmon data package.

This page is intentionally concise. For full package details or SPSR intake rules, use the linked canonical pages.

## Start here

1. **Map your columns to canonical terms**
   - Use [GC DFO Salmon Ontology (WIDOCO)](https://w3id.org/gcdfo/salmon) and the [metasalmon reusing-standards vignette](https://dfo-pacific-science.github.io/metasalmon/articles/reusing-standards-salmon-data-terms.html)
   - Record the **full** IRI for each mapped field
2. **Create a salmon data package**
   - Prefer the maintained `metasalmon` workflow: [5-Minute Quickstart](https://dfo-pacific-science.github.io/metasalmon/articles/metasalmon.html) and [Publishing Data Packages](https://dfo-pacific-science.github.io/metasalmon/articles/data-dictionary-publication.html)
   - Use the canonical layout: `metadata/` + `data/` with optional root `datapackage.json`
   - Use the [Salmon data package specification](salmon-data-exchange-package.qmd) when you need the field-by-field contract
3. **Review + validate before you upload or publish**
   - Complete the `metasalmon` review loop on the package folder
   - Run `metasalmon::validate_salmon_datapackage(pkg_path, require_iris = TRUE)` before downstream submission
4. **Choose your destination path**
   - SPSR / FSAR intake → [Salmon data package + guided review + SPSR intake path](ontology-data-package-gpt.qmd)
   - External release → [Publish Your Data](publish-data.qmd)

## Minimal standardization checklist

- [ ] scope and grain are clear
- [ ] identifiers, time fields, measurements, and controlled values are mapped
- [ ] canonical IRIs are recorded in `metadata/column_dictionary.csv`
- [ ] package structure follows `metadata/` + `data/`
- [ ] package-level validation passes before downstream submission

## Cookbook spotlight

If you are working with escapement data, use the focused recipe:

- [CU/composite escapement estimates for package-first SPSR intake](../../cookbook/cu-composite-escapement-estimates.qmd)

For a longer implementation walkthrough, use:

- [FSAR Data Standardization Workflow](../../how_to_guides/fsar-data-standardization-workflow.qmd)
- [FSAR to SPSR: End-to-End Example](../../how_to_guides/fsar-to-spsr-end-to-end-example.qmd)

## Next Steps

- [Salmon Data Integration System — Start Here](semantic-salmon-system.qmd)
- [Salmon data package specification](salmon-data-exchange-package.qmd)
- [Salmon data package + guided review + SPSR intake path](ontology-data-package-gpt.qmd)
- [Data Stewardship Unit contact](mailto:FADSDataStewardship-GestiondesdonneesSFDA@dfo-mpo.gc.ca)
